_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies PA as 'no document required' for ID, though first-time voters who registered by mail may need to show ID. Pennsylvania has no-excuse absentee/mail voting (Act 77, 2019) and no early in-person voting separate from absentee. PA's 2026 primary is typically the third Tuesday in May; TODO verify exact 2026 date. Registration deadline is 15 days before the election per PA law. PA does not have same-day registration.

earlyVoting.notes: Pennsylvania does not have traditional early in-person voting. No-excuse mail voting (absentee) is available. Mail ballot applications open as soon as the primary is announced.
Pennsylvania does not have traditional early in-person voting. No-excuse mail (absentee) voting is available for all registered voters.

Pennsylvania does not offer traditional early in-person voting. No-excuse vote-by-mail (absentee) is available for all registered voters. Mail ballot applications must be received by the county board of elections 7 days before the election.

acceptedIds: First-time voters who registered by mail and did not provide ID at registration must show a photo ID or a current utility bill, bank statement, paycheck, government check, or other government document with name and address
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Pennsylvania does not prohibit phones in polling places. Voters may not photograph their completed ballot. Electioneering is prohibited within 10 feet of a voting machine or within the polling place.
additionalRules: Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary. Unaffiliated voters may not vote in partisan primaries.
No-excuse mail (absentee) voting available for all registered voters since 2019 (Act 77).
Mail ballot applications must be received by the county board of elections at least 7 days before the election.
Mail ballots must be enclosed in the secrecy envelope and outer envelope to be counted.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request a mail ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee, enacted via Act 77 in 2019)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 7 days before the election (received by county board of elections)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county board of elections by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the county board of elections by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count. Must be returned in the inner secrecy envelope.
